text:
Once upon a winter there was a boy named Oliver. Deeply introspective, he lived most days lost in his thoughts. Every snowfall was a new adventure to him.

One snowy afternoon, feeling lonely because his usual companions were snowflakes, Oliver decided to make a snowman. He shaped a fuzzy snowball into a silly hat and a stick for a nose, topped it with a big round ball for eyes.

Later that day, a girl named Lucy appeared at his doorstep. She’d come from near by like a breeze through winter bark. Oliver invited her inside.

She told him stories of distant lands, animated with colors and life they’d never experienced. Oliver’s imagination soared.

Lucy taught him how to carve a hollowed-out root for a trunk, woven together with small branches. Together they built a cozy snow domicile. Oliver painted it with a rainbow streaked storm, feeling joy in every stroke.

They spent hours laughing as they played pretend. Oliver was the brave knight saving
